settings.siteDescription: We create simple, well-designed digital products, practical tools, creative experiments, and useful resources that prioritize clarity, usability, and craftsmanship.

about.title: The Philosophy
about.subtitle: We believe in order, clarity, and precision.
about.metaDescription: Learn about the core principles that guide our product design, engineering, and resources at FeelsNeat.
about.philosophyText: The web is often over-engineered. We design and build products that focus on what matters: speed, clarity, and utility. By choosing clean layout structures, standard coding patterns, and avoiding visual noise, we create software that behaves exactly as users expect.
about.teamText: We value craftsmanship, simplicity, usability, and continuous improvement. We believe in doing honest, focused work and publishing tools that provide real value.
about.content:
FeelsNeat exists because we enjoy the feeling of tidy, well-organized digital environments.

We don't build to follow temporary trends or promote complex frameworks. We build because we believe that digital experiences are better when they are simple and well-crafted. Everything we release—whether it is an internal developer tool, a small open-source package, a detailed observation article, or a physical resource—is designed to feel neat.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.subtitle: We would love to hear from you.
contact.metaDescription: Connect with the FeelsNeat team for project collaborations, feedback on our tools, or general inquiries.
contact.infoText: If you prefer direct emails or want to share feedback on any of our open-source tools, feel free to reach out. We read every message and respond thoughtfully.
contact.content: Whether you have questions about our experiments, want to collaborate on a digital product, or just want to say hello, we are always open to conversation.

home.title: FeelsNeat
home.subtitle: Simple, well-designed solutions create better experiences.
home.tagline: We design and build digital products, practical tools, creative experiments, and useful resources. Our focus is on clarity, craftsmanship, and usability.
home.observationsSubtitle: Notes, research, and reflections from the FeelsNeat journey, published on Substack.
home.content:
We believe that software should be quiet, fast, and helpful.

The modern digital world is often cluttered with unnecessary complexity and distracting marketing promises. We exist to build the opposite. We create utilities and products that do one thing cleanly, load instantly, and feel satisfying to use.

services.title: Visual Presentation Refresh
services.summary: We help digital product creators refresh and modernize the visual presentation of their products to improve marketplace appeal and brand trust.
services.content:
A great product deserves a high-quality visual representation. We collaborate with creators to refresh outdated mockups and product screenshots, creating modern presentation assets that align with contemporary standards.

Our process focuses on:

  • Mockup Modernization: Replacing outdated device frame wrappers with clean, timeless visual structures.
  • Marketplace Visuals: Optimizing store graphics, banner layouts, and feature callouts.
  • Product Screenshots: Improving clarity, readability, and context in screenshots.
  • Accents & Presentation Assets: Structuring custom graphic resources for newer devices, web showcases, or newsletters.

services.title: Clean Web Engineering
services.summary: We build fast, standards-compliant web interfaces utilizing lightweight architectures (Next.js, TypeScript) optimized for serverless edge deployment.
services.content:
We write clean, readable code with a focus on web performance and accessibility. We prefer lightweight, native solutions over heavy third-party framework dependencies.

Our engineering capabilities include:

  • Serverless & Edge Development: Building fast, globally distributed apps using Cloudflare Pages & Workers.
  • Standards-Compliant UI: Implementing accessible HTML5 structures and semantic CSS.
  • Type-Safe Systems: Constructing robust front-ends using TypeScript and Next.js.

services.title: Product & Tool Design
services.summary: We design and prototype minimal utilities, developer tools, and web experiments focused on doing one thing cleanly and well.
services.content:
We prototype and develop internal tools and standalone micro-products. We focus on utility and clean layouts that simplify complex tasks for creators and developers.

Our development principles:

  • Single-Purpose Utility: Crafting products that solve a single problem with absolute focus.
  • Minimal Configuration: Building tools that work cleanly with zero clutter.
  • Open Documentation: Providing clear, helpful explanations and guides.

work.title: Telemetry Dashboard Refresh
work.summary: Redesigning and modernizing the visual screenshots and presentation assets for a satellite telemetry interface.
work.content:
AeroSpace Tech creates complex software for satellite tracking. While their technical engine was highly capable, their marketplace listings and product mockups felt outdated, making it hard to build trust with new customers.

We redesigned their visual presentation layout:

  1. Mockup Design: Cleaned up the hardware frames, focusing strictly on the charts and visual data.
  2. Contrast & Colors: Adjusted the saturation to make text crisp and legible.
  3. Marketing Graphic Assets: Created high-quality presentation graphics showing key UI interactions.

work.title: CSS Grid Spacing Tool
work.summary: An interactive code experiment to visualize, test, and export clean CSS grid gaps and typography rhythms.
work.content:
As part of our commitment to building simple, practical utilities, we built an interactive layout sandbox.

This tool helps designers and developers:

  • Visualize vertical grid rhythms based on typographic line heights.
  • Drag and adjust column spacing with instant CSS output.
  • Export clean, zero-dependency Tailwind v4 styles.

observations.title: Tailwind CSS v4: Streamlining Styles
observations.author: Sanjai
observations.summary: An analysis of how Tailwind CSS v4 simplifies style management, improves compilation speed, and eliminates the config file.

observations.title: Why Web Speed is the Ultimate Aesthetic
observations.author: Sanjai
observations.summary: Performance isn't just a technical metric; it is an essential part of user experience and visual design. A site that loads instantly feels neat.
